comparison.title: Comparison of WZ (2).xml and Dd.txt on Demon-Related Themes
comparison.summary: This file summarizes the shared themes between Wizidagih? ? Z?dspram (WZ) and D?dest?n ? d?n?g (DD) around Druj, demons, Ahrimen, opposition, evil, sorcery, and punishment.

theme.name: Druj or Druz as hostile anti-cosmic force
theme.description: WZ presents druz as a reason for protective creation and as a major enemy entering the world; DD repeatedly presents Druz as adversary, corrupter, and destroyer.

theme.name: D?ws and Druz acting together
theme.description: Both texts pair demon powers with Druj or druwands.

theme.name: Pety?rag and opposition
theme.description: Both texts use the idea of hostile opposition; DD also emphasizes a-pety?rag?h as the purified final state.

theme.name: An?g as evil and affliction
theme.description: DD uses an?g?h very extensively; WZ also connects evil with destructive moral and eschatological states.

theme.name: Ahrimen or destructive spirit
theme.description: DD names Ahrimen explicitly many times; WZ also contains Ahrimen and angraman. In both works he is tied to evil, hell, or the anti-good principle.

theme.name: Sorcery and demon-backed corruption
theme.description: WZ links druz, magical harm, and demonic injury to creation. DD parallels this with ??d?g?n, du?-d?n figures, and demon-driven destruction.

theme.name: Du?-d?n and false religion
theme.description: This is especially strong in DD, but WZ also links demonic forces with corrupt teaching and moral corruption.

theme.name: Punishment of the druwand and final defeat of demonic opposition
theme.description: Both texts tie Druj to hell, punishment, and eventual removal in the final restoration.

comparison.conclusion: The strongest shared theme is that Druj is the active destructive and deceptive force allied with d?ws, Ahrimen, sorcery, false religion, affliction, and the fate of the druwand in hell, while final restoration is imagined as the removal of that demonic opposition.
